titleStmt.title: No. 864The Secretary of State to the Embassy in Jordan

div.head:
No. 864
The Secretary of State to the Embassy in Jordan
Sent also priority to Tel Aviv, Jerusalem, and Cairo; sent by pouch to Beirut, London, and Damascus.
div.p:
61. In light Dayan’s willingness urge Israel participation in meeting HKJ and Israel commanders Jerusalem sector late August (Deptel 67 to Tel AvivPrinted as telegram 38 to Amman, Document 853. and Tel Aviv 173 to DeptIn telegram 173 from Tel Aviv, Aug. 17, Russell reported that he had raised with Eytan the possibility of reactivating, improving, and extending the local commanders’ meetings. Eytan said he would prefer to postpone discussion until Dayan returned from Washington later in the week. (684A.85/8–1754)) and in view statement last para Jerusalem’s 45 to Dept.,In the last paragraph of telegram 45 from Jerusalem, Aug. 16, Cole reported that he had sounded out the senior Arab Legion officer in the Jerusalem area about local commanders’ meetings. The officer’s opinion was that such meetings were useful and could presumably be renewed whenever the Israelis signified a desire to do so. (684A.85/8–1654) request Amman’s and Tel Aviv’s views as to practicability their approaching governments soonest to urge such meeting. Should this approach be made without awaiting definitive result efforts arrange meeting Gaza?
Such an approach would be along lines final para Deptel 205 to CairoPrinted as telegram 38 to Amman, Document 853. with emphasis on limited nature agenda.
